text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions or methods, to ensure they work correctly on their own. Integration tests check how different parts of the system work together. One common approach is test-driven development (TDD), where you write a test before the actual code. You then write just enough code to pass the test. This cycle helps improve code quality and design. Overall, a good testing strategy builds confidence. Developers can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality, which leads to more reliable and maintainable software.
